easy.question: What is the aim of the Electrical Safety Management Policy?
easy.answer: To manage and ensure safety of fixed electrical installations (landlord, domestic and workplace supplies), protecting tenants, residents, employees and the public. :contentReference[oaicite:23]{index=23}

easy.question: Where does the policy apply?
easy.answer: All Midland Heart properties where we retain responsibility, including general needs, independent/retirement/specialist schemes (and communal areas), offices, and agency-managed/leased properties. :contentReference[oaicite:24]{index=24}

easy.question: What is an EICR and why is it used?
easy.answer: An Electrical Installation Condition Report identifies damage, defects and deterioration before they pose a risk. :contentReference[oaicite:25]{index=25}

easy.question: How often are fixed installations inspected/tested?
easy.answer: Domestic properties: at change of occupancy or every 5 years; communal parts and offices/workplaces: every 5 years; new domestic installs: 5 years. :contentReference[oaicite:26]{index=26}

easy.question: How often is PAT testing done?
easy.answer: Every 2 years in offices/other work locations and in communal areas/ furnished domestic tenancies. :contentReference[oaicite:27]{index=27}

easy.question: What contractor competencies are required?
easy.answer: Registration with NICEIC/NAPIT (or equivalent), Part P self-cert scheme, City & Guilds NVQ III and 2391/2394/2395 (or equivalent), competence with BS 7671 and related standards. :contentReference[oaicite:28]{index=28}

easy.question: How are concerns/complaints received and handled on social media?
easy.answer: Via any channel (staff, phone, email, social). Social media reports receive a private reply to protect confidentiality. :contentReference[oaicite:29]{index=29}

easy.question: What is Midland Heart’s stance when things go wrong?
easy.answer: Accept responsibility and put things right quickly. :contentReference[oaicite:30]{index=30}

medium.question: A tenant cannot do weekday access for EICR. What does the policy allow?
medium.answer: We will endeavour to make reasonable adjustments (e.g., flexible working patterns for contractors) and agree a suitable arrangement with the tenant. :contentReference[oaicite:31]{index=31}

medium.question: How does responsibility differ for leaseholders/shared owners?
medium.answer: Periodic domestic inspection/testing responsibility often remains with the leaseholder/shared owner; Midland Heart undertakes periodic checks for communal areas where we retain responsibility. :contentReference[oaicite:32]{index=32}

medium.question: List key elements of the Electrical Safety Operational Framework.
medium.answer: Planned repairs/maintenance/upgrades; FSLSS programme (tests for fire alarms, emergency lighting, automated doors); remedials within appropriate timescales; inspection/testing aligned to BS 7671 Guidance Note 3; robust records/quality monitoring; competency assurance. :contentReference[oaicite:33]{index=33}

medium.question: What assurance activities check contractor quality and competence?
medium.answer: External independent testing; continuous internal auditing; procurement/contract-management checks via a training matrix reviewed regularly (monthly). :contentReference[oaicite:34]{index=34}

medium.question: An advocate emails about a tenant’s electrical safety concern. Can you discuss details?
medium.answer: Only with the tenant’s written consent (MP/Councillor enquiries are exceptions that do not require written consent). :contentReference[oaicite:35]{index=35}

medium.question: What guidance standard sets inspection/testing frequency?
medium.answer: ‘Guidance Note 3’ of the current edition of IET Wiring Regulations (BS 7671). :contentReference[oaicite:36]{index=36}

medium.question: Which groups are covered by the policy beyond tenants?
medium.answer: Employees, members of the public and contractors working on our behalf within our buildings and homes. :contentReference[oaicite:37]{index=37}

medium.question: What related internal documents does the policy link to?
medium.answer: Electrical Safety Management Operational Framework, Electrical Technical Protocol, Health & Safety Policy, Reasonable Adjustment Policy, Safeguarding & Wellbeing Policy, Data Protection Policy, Code of Conduct for Contractors. :contentReference[oaicite:38]{index=38}

hard.question: Outline a compliance plan for a mixed estate with domestic, communal and office supplies coming due within 6 months—including remedials and records.
hard.answer: Programme EICRs for domestic dwellings (5-year/change of occupancy), 5-year tests for communal/office supplies, and 2-year PAT cycles; ensure remedial works are completed within appropriate timescales; retain robust records and quality monitoring; verify contractor competency (NICEIC/NAPIT etc.); and audit outcomes. :contentReference[oaicite:39]{index=39}

hard.question: A shared-owner’s flat has no recent EICR. What is Midland Heart responsible for?
hard.answer: A bespoke approach applies—domestic testing responsibility often remains with the leaseholder/shared owner; Midland Heart undertakes communal area checks where responsible. :contentReference[oaicite:40]{index=40}

hard.question: A social media post alleges unsafe electrics in a block. What’s the compliant response path?
hard.answer: Reply privately to protect confidentiality; log the concern; follow the inspection/testing programme; arrange access with reasonable adjustments; and communicate outcomes appropriately. :contentReference[oaicite:41]{index=41}

hard.question: You discover a contractor team lacks current C&G 2391/2394/2395 for testers. What actions align with policy?
hard.answer: Use procurement/contract-management checks to address competence gaps, require accredited qualifications/registrations, and use internal/external audits to assure quality. :contentReference[oaicite:42]{index=42}

hard.question: How does the FSLSS programme integrate with electrical compliance?
hard.answer: It includes tests of key electrically-powered life-safety components (fire alarms, emergency lighting, automated doors) alongside the broader electrical inspection/testing regime. :contentReference[oaicite:43]{index=43}

hard.question: After an incident, a tenant demands disclosure via an advocate. What must be verified?
hard.answer: Obtain written consent before discussing the case with the advocate; otherwise communicate directly with the tenant, respecting GDPR and Data Protection procedures. :contentReference[oaicite:44]{index=44}

hard.question: Set a 24-month PAT schedule for mixed spaces and justify it.
hard.answer: PAT is required every 2 years in offices/other work locations and in communal areas/furnished domestic tenancies, aligning with the policy’s frequency table. :contentReference[oaicite:45]{index=45}
